content:
Member-only story

Compiler
The most useful AI no longer looks like AI

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Styled photograph of an empty home office at first light: a clean desk, one laptop with a faintly glowing screen, soft morning light through half-open blinds, dust motes suspended, no people, quiet uncanny stillness. [Conceptualized by , AI-generated image.] Made by Midjourney.com, 2026.The most profound technologies are those that disappear. They weave themselves into the fabric of everyday life until they are indistinguishable from it. — Mark Weiser, The Computer for the 21st Century, Scientific American, September 1991.
These are thoughts from the moments when I tried to reclaim the pen. I wrote this a few weeks back, when it felt like the AI was finishing more of my sentences than I was. If you have ever felt the same, I’d love to hear your take.
Not Deeply, Only Daily
By morning, the inbox is clean.
Something came in the night
and carried off the lottery,
the stranger with a fortune to move,
the last chance, the final notice —
swept to a room I’ll never find.
It had a face once —
a paperclip, two eyebrows
folded in the corner of the glass:
It looks like you’re writing a letter?
